description: General-purpose test case templates matched against a selected swagger endpoint at runtime.

templates.purpose: Return paginated list with default parameters
templates.notes: No query params — API uses its own defaults for page and size

templates.purpose: Return full detail for an existing record by valid ID
templates.notes: Use a known seeded or previously created ID

templates.purpose: Successfully create a new resource with all required fields
templates.notes: Verify the response body contains the created resource with a generated ID

templates.purpose: Successfully replace an existing resource with valid data
templates.notes: Confirm updated values are reflected in a subsequent GET

templates.purpose: Successfully apply a partial update to an existing resource
templates.notes: Only changed fields should differ; unchanged fields must remain intact

templates.purpose: Successfully delete an existing resource by valid ID
templates.notes: APIs return 200 (with body) or 204 (no content); follow up with GET to confirm the resource is gone (expect 404)

templates.purpose: Return filtered list when a valid keyword matches existing records
templates.notes: keyword=<known value> — result should be a non-empty subset of all records

templates.purpose: Return correct page when explicit page and size params are provided
templates.notes: e.g. page=2&size=5 — verify total count and items are consistent

templates.purpose: Return list sorted in ascending order by a supported field
templates.notes: e.g. sort=name,asc — verify the response order matches

templates.purpose: Return list sorted in descending order by a supported field
templates.notes: e.g. sort=name,desc — verify the response order is reversed

templates.purpose: Successfully create a resource with all optional fields populated
templates.notes: Verify all optional fields are persisted and returned in the response

templates.purpose: Successfully create a resource with only required fields (omitting all optional ones)
templates.notes: Verify optional fields default to null or their defined default values

templates.purpose: Update a single field while leaving all other fields unchanged
templates.notes: Confirm only the targeted field changes; others remain as-is

templates.purpose: Update multiple fields in a single request
templates.notes: Verify all updated fields are reflected; unrelated fields are not affected

templates.purpose: Reject request when required body fields are missing
templates.notes: Send empty body {} — expect 400 or 422 with field-level error messages in response

templates.purpose: Reject request when field values have invalid types
templates.notes: e.g. numeric field = 'abc', boolean field = 'yes' — expect 400 or 422

templates.purpose: Reject request when numeric fields are out of allowed range
templates.notes: e.g. price = -1, quantity = 999999999 — expect 400 or 422

templates.purpose: Reject request when string fields exceed maximum allowed length
templates.notes: Send a string of 1000+ characters for a name or description field — expect 400 or 422

templates.purpose: Reject request with malformed JSON body
templates.notes: Send Content-Type: application/json with invalid JSON syntax

templates.purpose: Return 404 when the requested resource ID does not exist
templates.notes: Use a large non-existent ID, e.g. 999999999

templates.purpose: Reject the request when the path param ID has an invalid format
templates.notes: Use a non-numeric or special-character ID, e.g. 'abc' or '@#$' — APIs return 400 (bad format) or 404 (no such route/resource)

templates.purpose: Reject creation of a duplicate resource that violates a unique constraint
templates.notes: POST the same payload twice — second request should return 409 Conflict

templates.purpose: Return 405 when an HTTP method is not allowed on the endpoint
templates.notes: Send a method the endpoint does not support, e.g. DELETE on a read-only resource or PATCH where only GET/POST are allowed

templates.purpose: Reject request when Authorization header is absent
templates.notes: Send request with no Authorization header at all

templates.purpose: Reject request when the token is malformed or tampered
templates.notes: Authorization: Bearer invalid_token_xyz

templates.purpose: Reject request when the token has expired
templates.notes: Use a real token past its expiry timestamp

templates.purpose: Reject request from a user with a valid token but insufficient permissions
templates.notes: Use a token for a low-privilege role that should not access this endpoint

templates.purpose: Reject access to a resource owned by another user
templates.notes: Use a valid token for User A but request a resource belonging to User B

templates.purpose: Return empty list (not 404) when no records match the search criteria
templates.notes: keyword=zzznomatch999 — data array should be [] with total=0

templates.purpose: Handle page number beyond total pages gracefully
templates.notes: page=9999 — should return empty list, not an error

templates.purpose: Return all records on one page when size equals or exceeds total count
templates.notes: size=1000 — all records on one page; verify total matches

templates.purpose: Accept request when string fields are exactly at the maximum allowed length
templates.notes: Send a string of exactly maxLength characters — should succeed

templates.purpose: Accept request when numeric fields are exactly at the minimum allowed value
templates.notes: e.g. price = 0 (if 0 is the defined minimum) — should succeed

templates.purpose: Handle extremely large request payload without crashing
templates.notes: Send a body with hundreds of extra unknown fields — expect 400 or 413 (Payload Too Large), not 500
